Ron DeSantis. The five “issues” committees are Legislative (work with Congress for federal antitrust protection), Rules (determine NIL, portal, eligibility standards), NCAA Reform (future governance), Media (media rights and SBA), and Player-Agent relationship issues.

Commissioners from the SEC, ACC, Big 12, Big Ten and American and Notre Dame AD Pete Bevacqua are assigned to Rules, Media and NCAA Reform committees as well as many other notable names, including Nick Saban, Condoleezza Rice and Adam Silver.

title: Sky Sports: Liverpool must pay £87m to sign electric forward
description: Leipzig valuation puts Diomande among elite targetsLiverpool and Man Utd have been placed firmly on alert after RB Leipzig set a striking £87.3 million (€100m) valuation on highly-rated winger Yan ...
fullText:
Liverpool and Man Utd have been placed firmly on alert after RB Leipzig set a striking £87.3 million (€100m) valuation on highly-rated winger Yan Diomande. According to reports originating from Sky in Germany, the Bundesliga club are prepared to entertain offers at that level, even as they privately hope to retain the 19-year-old for at least another season.

Leipzig’s stance reflects both Diomande’s explosive rise and the premium now attached to young, high-output wide players. The club are understood to be working on a new contract that would reward his performances with improved terms. Crucially, however, any new agreement would not include a release clause — a clear indication Leipzig intend to retain control over his future.

The original source notes: “RB Leipzig have slapped a £87.3m (€100m) price tag” on the winger, underlining the scale of investment required for any suitor to prise him away.

Liverpool’s interest in Diomande is neither casual nor speculative. The Merseyside club are actively assessing attacking reinforcements ahead of a significant summer reshuffle, with the departure of Mohamed Salah marking the end of an era at Anfield.

Diomande’s profile aligns with Liverpool’s evolving recruitment model: young, dynamic, and already producing at a high level in a competitive league. The Ivory Coast international has delivered 11 goals and eight assists in 29 appearances this season, numbers that place him among Europe’s most productive emerging wide forwards.

The original report highlights that Liverpool are “closely monitoring” the player and remain in dialogue with his representatives. That ongoing contact suggests groundwork is already being laid, should the club decide to accelerate their pursuit.

From a tactical perspective, Diomande offers directness, pace, and versatility across the front line — attributes that would suit Liverpool’s high-intensity attacking structure. His ability to carry the ball into dangerous areas and contribute consistently in the final third makes him a natural candidate to fill the void left by Salah’s exit.

Manchester United are unlikely to allow Liverpool a clear run at Diomande. Sky Sports News indicates that Man Utd have emerged as one of the clubs showing the strongest interest, positioning themselves as serious contenders in what is shaping up to be a fiercely competitive transfer race.

United’s need for attacking reinforcement is well documented, particularly in wide areas where consistency has been lacking. Diomande’s output and upward trajectory make him an attractive solution, especially given his capacity to influence matches both as a scorer and creator.

Competition extends beyond England. Bayern Munich, Paris Saint-Germain, and Tottenham have all tracked Diomande’s development, further inflating both his profile and potential transfer fee. In such a crowded market, decisive action and financial commitment will likely determine the outcome.

Diomande’s emergence is no accident. At just 19, he combines physical maturity with technical sharpness, a blend that has accelerated his impact at senior level. His performances for Leipzig this season have not only driven his club’s attacking output but also elevated his standing on the international stage.

He is expected to feature prominently for Ivory Coast at this summer’s World Cup, a platform that could further enhance his reputation — and potentially his valuation. For Liverpool and Man Utd, that adds urgency to any transfer considerations.

The original source emphasises that Diomande is “set to be one of the most coveted wingers this summer,” a statement that feels entirely justified given both his numbers and the calibre of clubs circling.

Ultimately, this is shaping into a classic modern transfer narrative: a breakout talent, a protective selling club, and multiple elite suitors ready to test financial limits. Whether Liverpool or Man Utd emerge victorious may depend not only on money, but on vision — and which club can best convince Diomande he is central to their future.

title: The hero of the day
description: The earliest bits of concrete NC State sports memories I have involve Torry Holt and Justin Gainey—1997 felt a little transformative for both sports at the time. Torry helped the football team pull a huge upset in a (nonconference) game at Syracuse to open the ‘97 season; in March of that year, Gainey played every […]
fullText:
The earliest bits of concrete NC State sports memories I have involve Torry Holt and Justin Gainey—1997 felt a little transformative for both sports at the time. Torry helped the football team pull a huge upset in a (nonconference) game at Syracuse to open the ‘97 season; in March of that year, Gainey played every minute of a surprise run to the ACC tournament championship.

The ACC tournament was still a tidy nine-team venture at that point, with the play-in game on Thursday night. This being the deeply depressing post-V era, NC State lived in that game, and man am I glad that’s the extent of what I remember about it. Like so much from back then, it feels terribly quaint that you’d just sacrifice a major program at the altar of some NCAA bullshit. But that’s what NC State did in the 1990s.

Justin Gainey was a pioneer in the school’s initial revival effort—Is winning allowed? We’re allowed to do this?—and he was an immediate impact player for Herb Sendek’s nascent program.

After each State win in that ACC tournament, my dad and his friends cranked “Hero Of The Day” by Metallica, which became a good luck charm, because you can’t change anything as long as it’s working during ACC tournament weekend.

A lot changes over time, but many things don’t. This is still the only Metallica song I can tolerate, for instance. Justin Gainey worked more than one miracle that weekend.
